mnemonics: Men Are Lovely, But Not Always Connected (Local)
Mike Always Looks For The Last Arc (Arc-wise)
keyPoints: In a metric space, a set is locally connected if for every point in the set and every open ball around that point, there is an open connected set contained within that ball.
A space is arc-wise connected if for every two points in the space, there exists a continuous function from the unit interval to the space that maps the endpoints of the interval to those two points.
Local connectedness is a weaker condition than arc-wise connectedness, and it is not necessarily true that a space that is arc-wise connected is also locally connected.
